generation.method: checked runtime inventory plus exact npm tarballs extracted into a temporary node_modules tree
generation.probeIsolation: one Node child process per React DOM subpath, condition, and mode

probeModes.reason: Default Node development captures the CommonJS package facade, descriptor shape, and development export values.
Default Node production captures the same public subpaths with production CJS bundles selected by NODE_ENV.
The react-server condition narrows the root facade and makes client, server, static, and profiling branches throw.
Production react-server branches are recorded separately because they load production throwing modules.

conditionModes.reason: Custom browser condition as resolved by Node; Node's built-in node condition may still win.
Custom worker condition as resolved by Node for aggregate server and static subpaths.
Custom edge-light condition as resolved by Node; export-map order is captured as evidence.
Custom workerd condition as resolved by Node.
Custom bun condition as resolved by Node; server chooses the Bun wrapper while static has no bun branch.
Custom deno condition as resolved by Node.

blockedSubpathModes.reason: Baseline Node proves physical root .js files and CJS implementation files are not public package subpaths.
The react-server condition still respects package exports before any physical implementation file can be loaded.

intentionalGaps.reason: This oracle records pinned React DOM package behavior only; Fast React React DOM scaffolds are owned by a separate worker and are not required here.
Browser, worker, edge-light, workerd, bun, and deno modes are Node resolver evidence with custom conditions, not execution in those runtimes.
Client roots, portals, resource dispatch, Fizz, static prerendering, hydration, DOM mutation, and event behavior are outside this export oracle.
